canonical_question: What is the realistic total cost of ownership for a partner/outsource scenario?
aliases: partner scenario total cost of ownership
constraints: TCO multipliers vary dramatically by engagement type — staff augmentation, managed services, and SI engagements have fundamentally different cost structures
Regional labor arbitrage savings (40-70% on hourly rates) can be entirely consumed by coordination overhead, rework, and governance costs
Framework produces estimates, not guarantees — actual TCO depends on vendor maturity, contract structure, and scope stability
Hidden costs only discovered 12-18 months into engagement — prospective TCO estimates are inherently uncertain
Assumes accurate scope definition as prerequisite, which is itself a common failure point driving change orders

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs the master build vs buy vs partner decision framework, not TCO analysis
User is evaluating specific vendor selection criteria for enterprise software
User needs to understand TCO for a build (in-house) scenario specifically

related_to.label: Build vs Buy vs Partner Decision Tree
Build vs Buy for Enterprise Software

often_confused_with.label: Build vs Buy for Integration Layer (architecture decision, not TCO analysis)

inputs_needed.question: What type of partner/outsource engagement is the user evaluating?
options: Staff augmentation — supplementing internal team with external developers
Managed services — outsourcing an entire function (IT ops, security, infrastructure)
System integrator — large-scale implementation project (ERP, platform migration)
General outsourcing TCO — needs the full framework across engagement types
